title: Heart-brain axis dysregulation in PTSD mice: Vagal-mediated insular cortex hyperactivity and its reversal by propranolol.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND: While abnormal brain connectivity is central to PTSD, sympathetically-driven cardiac dysfunction also occurs. Given the established role of cardiac modulation of brain function and its implication in psychiatric disorders, this study aims to delineate the role of the heart-brain connection in the mechanisms underlying PTSD. While abnormal brain connectivity is central to PTSD, sympathetically-driven cardiac dysfunction also occurs. Given the established role of cardiac modulation of brain function and its implication in psychiatric disorders, this study aims to delineate the role of the heart-brain connection in the mechanisms underlying PTSD. METHODS: A single prolonged stress (SPS)-induced male C57BL/6J mouse model of PTSD was employed. Anxiety- and fear-like behaviors were assessed using behavioral tests.
